headline: New Technology Transforms Plastic Waste Into Jet Fuel
meta_description: Chinese researchers develop a low-cost method to convert waste plastics into aviation fuel, offering a potential solution to growing plastic pollution.
summary_short: Chinese researchers have developed technology to convert waste plastics into aviation fuel at low cost, addressing the challenge of global plastic waste.
body:
The world produces more than 460 million tonnes of plastic annually—a staggering figure that highlights why scientists are racing to find solutions for waste that resists natural degradation.

Now, Chinese researchers have proposed a promising answer: technology that transforms discarded plastics directly into aviation fuel. The breakthrough addresses a dual crisis—plastic pollution and fuel demands—in one process.

But the path from laboratory success to real-world application remains uncertain. The current performance data exists only at laboratory scale, and researchers acknowledge that significant work must happen before the technology can scale up to industrial reactors. Practical hurdles still stand between this hopeful innovation and its deployment in factories worldwide.

Still, the research represents a meaningful step forward in reimagining waste as a resource rather than a burden.
